normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def sortSlice (s : Array Int) : Array Int := s.qsort (· ≤ ·)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the intended function that returns a sorted array (nondecreasing order) from the input slice.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem sortSlice_sorted (s : Array Int) : Sorted (· ≤ ·) (sortSlice s) := by
  simpa [sortSlice] using s.qsort_sorted (· ≤ ·)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem sortSlice_perm (s : Array Int) : sortSlice s ~ s := by
  simpa [sortSlice] using s.qsort_perm (· ≤ ·)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output contains exactly the same multiset of elements as the input (i.e., it is a permutation of the input).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem sortSlice_multiset (s : Array Int) : (sortSlice s).toMultiset = s.toMultiset := by
  simpa [Array.toMultiset] using (sortSlice_perm s).toMultiset_eq
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Equivalently, the output and input have equal multisets of elements.
